all.en: Min-jun carried the broken headphones back to the electronics store the next morning.
all.zh: 敏俊隔天早上把壞掉的耳機帶回電子產品店。

all.en: He had only used them for three days before the left speaker stopped working.
all.zh: 他才使用了三天，左邊的喇叭就壞了。

all.en: The salesperson at the counter examined the headphones with a suspicious expression.
all.zh: 櫃台的店員以懷疑的表情檢查這副耳機。

all.en: She insisted that the damage looked like it had been caused by the customer.
all.zh: 她堅稱這個損壞看起來是顧客造成的。

all.en: Min-jun calmly showed her the receipt and explained that the product was clearly defective.
all.zh: 敏俊冷靜地拿出收據，並解釋這個產品明顯有瑕疵。

all.en: He politely asked to speak with the store manager to resolve the issue.
all.zh: 他禮貌地要求和店經理談談以解決問題。

all.en: The manager arrived and listened to both sides of the story carefully.
all.zh: 經理走過來仔細聆聽雙方的說法。

all.en: After testing the headphones himself, he agreed that there was a clear manufacturing fault.
all.zh: 在親自測試耳機後，他同意這是明顯的製造瑕疵。

all.en: He offered Min-jun a full refund or an exchange for a newer model.
all.zh: 他提供敏俊全額退款，或是換一台更新的型號。

all.en: Min-jun chose the refund because he wanted to compare prices at other shops first.
all.zh: 敏俊選擇了退款，因為他想先到其他店家比較價格。

all.en: He left the store feeling satisfied that standing his ground had paid off.
all.zh: 他離開店家時感到滿意，堅持立場是值得的。

all.en: Outside the store, he wrote a quick note to remind himself of the experience.
all.zh: 在店外，他快速寫了一張紙條，提醒自己這次的經驗。

all.en: He realized that polite firmness was much more effective than getting angry.
all.zh: 他體會到，禮貌而堅定的態度遠比生氣有用得多。

all.en: That afternoon, he visited two other shops to compare similar headphone models.
all.zh: 那天下午，他到另外兩家店比較類似的耳機型號。

all.en: One shop offered a longer warranty period, which felt like much better value.
all.zh: 其中一家店提供了較長的保固期，感覺價值好上許多。

all.en: Min-jun also checked online reviews before making any new purchase decision.
all.zh: 敏俊在做出任何新的購買決定前，也查看了網路評價。

all.en: He chose a pair that cost a little more but came with stronger build quality.
all.zh: 他選了一副稍貴一點，但做工更扎實的耳機。

all.en: At home, he kept the new receipt safely inside a small folder for documents.
all.zh: 回到家後，他把新收據妥善地放在一個小型文件夾中。

all.en: He told his younger sister always to keep proof of purchase for everything.
all.zh: 他告訴妹妹，買任何東西都要保留購買證明。

all.en: His sister joked that he sounded like their cautious and careful mother.
all.zh: 妹妹開玩笑說，他講話的口氣像極了他們謹慎細心的媽媽。

all.en: Min-jun simply smiled, knowing that being a smart consumer protected his hard-earned money.
all.zh: 敏俊只是笑了笑，他知道當個聰明的消費者能保護他辛苦賺來的錢。

all.en: Min-jun started keeping a digital folder of every receipt and warranty card he received.
all.zh: 敏俊開始用電子資料夾，保存他收到的每一張收據和保固卡。

all.en: His careful habit helped him quickly solve another problem with a faulty kitchen appliance.
all.zh: 他謹慎的習慣，幫他迅速解決了另一台故障廚房家電的問題。

all.en: He politely explained the issue to the manager, who refunded him without any argument.
all.zh: 他禮貌地向經理說明問題，經理二話不說就把錢退給了他。

all.en: He realized that calm and respectful communication often produced the best customer service results.
all.zh: 他體會到，冷靜又尊重的溝通，通常能帶來最好的客服處理結果。

all.en: He decided to teach a short workshop at his community center about consumer rights.
all.zh: 他決定在社區中心開一場關於消費者權益的小型講座。

all.en: Many neighbors attended and asked questions about return policies and warranty time limits.
all.zh: 許多鄰居都來參加，詢問退換貨政策和保固期限相關的問題。

all.en: An elderly lady thanked him for explaining things in such clear and patient language.
all.zh: 一位年長女士感謝他用如此清楚又耐心的語言解釋這些事情。

all.en: She had been afraid to ask for refunds before, but his words gave her courage.
all.zh: 她以前不敢開口要求退款，但他的話給了她勇氣。

all.en: Min-jun felt proud knowing he had passed useful knowledge on to others around him.
all.zh: 敏俊感到驕傲，因為他把實用的知識傳遞給了身邊的人。

all.en: A small refund had grown into a quiet movement for smarter shopping inside his community.
all.zh: 一筆小小的退款，已悄悄發展為他社區裡一場聰明消費的運動。
